failure_surface: Agent incorrectly used openpyxl cell attribute 'is_formula' which does not exist in the library API, causing AttributeError during execution.
root_cause: The agent assumed openpyxl Cell objects have an 'is_formula' attribute similar to other spreadsheet libraries, but openpyxl requires checking cell value directly against string patterns or using data_type property to identify formulas.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Replace non-existent 'cell.is_formula' checks with proper openpyxl pattern matching: either inspect if cell.value starts with '=' or check cell.data_type == 'f' for formula cells.
evidence: First attempt code: print(f"C{row[0].value}: {c_cell.value} (Formula: {c_cell.is_formula})") triggered AttributeError: 'Cell' object has no attribute 'is_formula'
Second attempt corrected by removing is_formula check and instead checking if cell.value starts with '=' during verification
openpyxl documentation uses 'data_type' attribute ('f' for formulas) rather than 'is_formula' boolean flag

memory_items.title: openpyxl Formula Detection Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When checking if a cell contains a formula in openpyxl, do not use is_formula() method as it does not exist; instead check cell.data_type == 'f' or test if str(cell.value).startswith('=')
memory_items.generalization: Always verify library-specific cell inspection methods before using them; different spreadsheet APIs expose formula information through different properties

memory_items.title: Iterative Debugging Strategy
memory_items.lesson: When encountering AttributeError on unfamiliar attributes, trace back to library documentation rather than assuming method availability from prior experience with other tools
memory_items.generalization: Library attribute assumptions should be validated programmatically or through documentation review before coding execution paths

memory_items.title: Verification Code Robustness
memory_items.lesson: Write defensive verification code that handles missing attributes gracefully and provides alternative detection methods when primary approaches fail
memory_items.generalization: Include fallback strategies in validation routines when multiple equivalent detection patterns exist across spreadsheet manipulation contexts

edits.content:
## Library-Specific Attribute Awareness
- **Formula Detection**: In openpyxl, detect formulas by checking `cell.data_type == 'f'` or whether `str(cell.value).startswith('=')`; avoid using non-existent `.is_formula` attribute.
- **Attribute Verification**: Before iterating over cell objects, confirm available properties using `dir()` or official documentation to prevent AttributeError during runtime.
- **Fallback Validation**: When verifying formulas across multiple rows/columns, implement redundant detection patterns to handle edge cases where primary checks may fail.
